title: ferray-core: no WRITEBACKIFCOPY pattern for safe out-parameter mutation
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's WRITEBACKIFCOPY flag allows a function to work on a temporary contiguous copy and automatically write it back to the original non-contiguous array on completion (or discard on error). This is how ufuncs handle non-contiguous output arrays safely. ferray has no equivalent — functions either require contiguous input or silently return a new allocation. A writeback guard type (like a Drop-based scope guard that copies the temporary back to the original on success) would enable safe out-parameter patterns for non-contiguous arrays.
